content: By 2025, self-supervised autonomous agents can evolve a layered intrinsic curiosity framework that repurposes their working memory as a 'curiosity search engine' that prioritizes unobserved patterns by dynamically weighting novelty from both ICM (prediction error) and RND (random network distillation) signals. This memory-weighted curiosity mechanism lets the agent self-supervise its own learning curriculum without external reward engineering, adapting its exploration to the complexity of the environment it encounters.
